heading: Process to update labeling for certain generic drugs
label: 353d Process to update labeling for certain generic drugs

children.label: A
children.text: for which there are no unexpired patents included in the list under and no unexpired period of exclusivity;section 355(j)(7) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: for which the approval of the application has been withdrawn for reasons other than safety or effectiveness; and

children.label: I
children.text: there is new scientific evidence available pertaining to new or existing conditions of use that is not reflected in the approved labeling;

children.label: II
children.text: the approved labeling does not reflect current legal and regulatory requirements for content or format; or

children.label: III
children.text: there is a relevant accepted use in clinical practice that is not reflected in the approved labeling; and

children.label: ii
children.text: updating the approved labeling would benefit the public health.

The term “period of exclusivity”, with respect to a drug approved under , means any period of exclusivity under clause (ii), (iii), or (iv) of , clause (ii), (iii), or (iv) of , or section 355a, 355f, or 360cc of this title.section 355(c) of this titlesection 355(c)(3)(E) of this titlesection 355(j)(5)(F) of this title
The term “generic version” means a drug approved under whose reference listed drug is a covered drug.section 355(j) of this title

The term “relevant accepted use” means a use for a drug in clinical practice that is supported by scientific evidence that appears to the Secretary to meet the standards for approval under .section 355 of this title
The term “selected drug” means a covered drug for which the Secretary has determined through the process under subsection (c) that the labeling should be changed.
Enter into cooperative agreements or contracts with public or private entities to review the available scientific evidence concerning such drugs.

children.label: A
children.text: holding one or more public meetings;

children.label: B
children.text: opening a public docket for the submission of public comments; or

children.label: C
children.text: other means, as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Selection of drugs for updating
children.label: c Selection of drugs for updating
children.text: If the Secretary determines, with respect to a covered drug, that the available scientific evidence meets the standards under for adding or modifying information to the labeling or providing supplemental information to the labeling regarding the use of the covered drug, the Secretary may initiate the process under subsection (d).section 355 of this title

children.heading: Initiation of the process of updating
children.label: d Initiation of the process of updating

children.text: summarizes the findings supporting the determination of the Secretary that the available scientific evidence meets the standards under for adding or modifying information or providing supplemental information to the labeling of the covered drug pursuant to subsection (c);section 355 of this title

provides a clear statement regarding the additional, modified, or supplemental information for such labeling, according to the determination by the Secretary (including, as applicable, modifications to add the relevant accepted use to the labeling of the drug as an additional indication for the drug); and
states whether the statement under paragraph (2) applies to the selected drug as a class of covered drugs or only to a specific drug product.
agree to change the approved labeling to reflect the additional, modified, or supplemental information the Secretary has determined to be appropriate; or

notify the Secretary that the holder of the approved application does not believe that the requested labeling changes are warranted and submit a statement detailing the reasons why such changes are not warranted.

children.heading: Review of application holder’s response
children.label: f Review of application holder’s response

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Upon receipt of the application holder’s response, the Secretary shall promptly review each statement received under subsection (e)(2) and determine which labeling changes pursuant to the Secretary’s notice under subsection (d) are appropriate, if any. If the Secretary disagrees with the reasons why such labeling changes are not warranted, the Secretary shall provide opportunity for discussions with the application holders to reach agreement on whether the labeling for the covered drug should be updated to reflect available scientific evidence, and if so, the content of such labeling changes.

children.label: A
children.text: update its paper labeling for the drug at the next printing of that labeling;

children.label: B
children.text: update any electronic labeling for the drug within 30 days of such order; and

children.label: C
children.text: submit the revised labeling through the form, “Supplement—Changes Being Effected”.

children.heading: Violation
children.label: g Violation
children.text: If the holder of an approved application for the generic version of the selected drug does not comply with the requirements of subsection (f)(2), such generic version of the selected drug shall be deemed to be misbranded under .section 352 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to any labeling change required under this section, the generic version shall be deemed to have the same conditions of use and the same labeling as its reference listed drug for purposes of clauses (i) and (v) of . Any labeling change so required shall not have any legal effect for the applicant that is different than the legal effect that would have resulted if a supplemental application had been submitted and approved to conform the labeling of the generic version to a change in the labeling of the reference drug.section 355(j)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Supplemental applications
children.label: 2 Supplemental applications
children.text: Changes to labeling made in accordance with this section shall not be eligible for an exclusivity period under this chapter.

children.heading: Selection of drugs
children.label: 3 Selection of drugs
children.text: The Secretary shall not identify a drug as a covered drug or select a drug label for updating under subsection (b) or (c) solely based on the availability of new safety information. Upon identification of a drug as a covered drug under subsection (b), the Secretary may then consider the availability of new safety information (as defined in ) in determining whether the drug is a selected drug and in determining what labeling changes are appropriate.section 355–1(b) of this title

children.heading: Approval standards
children.label: 1 Approval standards
children.text: This section shall not be construed as altering the applicability of the standards for approval of an application under . No order shall be issued under this subsection unless the scientific evidence supporting the changed labeling meets the standards for approval applicable to any change to labeling under .section 355 of this titlesection 355 of this title

children.heading: Removal of information
children.label: 2 Removal of information
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to give the Secretary additional authority to remove approved indications for drugs, other than the authority described in this section.

children.heading: Secretary authority
children.label: 3 Secretary authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of the Secretary to require labeling changes under section 355() of this title.o

children.heading: Maintenance of labeling
children.label: 4 Maintenance of labeling
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the responsibility of the holder of an approved application under to maintain its labeling in accordance with existing requirements, including subpart B of part 201 and sections 314.70 and 314.97 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).section 355(j) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the number of covered drugs and description of the types of drugs the Secretary has selected for labeling changes and the rationale for such recommended changes; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of times the Secretary entered into discussions concerning a disagreement with an application holder or holders and a summary of the decision regarding a labeling change, if any; and
includes any recommendations of the Secretary for modifying the program under this section.
